body_txt: Since the 19th century, the boroughs of New York City , or the No Communities Were Harmed versions, have been characterized as having some of the toughest, angriest people you will ever meet. Perhaps it was all the immigrants clashing against each other. Perhaps it was all the gangs that sprouted in the poorer neighborhoods. Whatever the reason, anyone from The Big Rotten Apple is someone you don't want to mess with. The type of character can vary, often a badass with a Hair-Trigger Temper . Will sometimes be part of The Mafia , and possess the stereotypical attitude of a New York cab driver, at least in fiction. Even if he's one of the good guys, someone with a Brooklyn accent is bound to be tough nonetheless. Often tied closely to Joisey due to proximity, and both the Italian-American Caricature and its Old World counterpart the Rambunctious Italian due to the New York metropolitan area having the largest concentration of those of Italian descent in the US. The Big Rotten Apple might be a reason for all that pent-up anger. The Southern-Fried Private and Half-Witted Hillbilly are perhaps the rural/ Deep South versions of this trope. Overseas, compare the Crass Canuck , Southies , The Idiot from Osaka , Violent Glaswegian , Oop North , Dublin Skanger , Football Hooligans , The Quincy Punk , Fighting Irish , Northern Irish and Nasty , Amoral Afrikaner , The Bogan , Asian Rudeness , and French Jerk . Contrast Minnesota Nice , Sweet Home Alabama , and The Friendly Texan for different parts of America with more friendly dispositions.
